macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Climb on Optimism Over Imminent End to Iran War
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose 0.7% and Nasdaq 100 gained 1% amid President Trump's comments forecasting US exit from the Iran conflict in two to three weeks, boosting market sentiment. Brent crude fell around 2.8% below $100 a barrel on war-end hopes, though oil remains elevated due to Strait of Hormuz closure.

items.title: Oil Prices Slide but Remain High Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure Risks
items.summary: Brent crude dropped 2.8% on de-escalation optimism, but prices are up 50-64% since late February Iran attacks, with natural gas up 8% in US and 59% in Europe. Analysts warn prolonged closure could sustain high energy costs, pressuring consumers and inflation.

items.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady in March Meeting Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: The Federal Reserve left interest rates unchanged for the second consecutive meeting on March 18, as oil shocks from Iran war fueled inflation fears. Two-year Treasury yield rose to 3.81% following strong US labor and retail sales data.

items.title: S&P 500 Ends Q1 Down 5.1% on Oil Shock and War Concerns
items.summary: US stocks closed Q1 with S&P 500 down 5.1%—its worst month since May 2025—driven by record oil price surge from Iran conflict, entering correction territory. Rally on March 31 followed Trump's war-end signals.

items.title: February Retail Sales Up 0.6% But Iran War Poses Consumer Risk
items.summary: US retail sales increased 0.6% in February, supported by solid labor data, but predate Iran war escalation. Morgan Stanley cut consumption forecasts by 30 basis points due to oil shock impacting low- and middle-income spending.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Rises 1.3% to $2.35T Amid Extreme Fear
items.summary: The total cryptocurrency market capitalization increased by 1.3% to $2.35T in the past 24 hours, recovering to last Friday's levels despite the Fear and Greed Index dropping to 8 in extreme fear territory. Algorand led gains with over 22%, while Bitcoin traded up 1.5% at $68.4K.

items.title: Bitcoin Reclaims $68,000 on Iran Ceasefire Hopes
items.summary: Bitcoin surged back above $68,000 as hopes for an Iran ceasefire drove a risk-on rally, with crypto markets rising alongside retreating oil prices under $100 per barrel. The rebound follows support at recent lows around $60K.

items.title: Algorand Surges 22% as Top Performer in Recovering Market
items.summary: Algorand gained over 22% in the past 24 hours, standing out amid general market stagnation and a broader crypto rebound.

items.title: Google Analysts Warn Quantum Computers Could Hack Bitcoin Sooner Than Expected
items.summary: Google analysts indicate quantum computers may hack Bitcoin with less computing power than thought, potentially endangering nearly a third of existing BTC.

items.title: Nakamoto Sells 284 BTC at 40% Loss Amid Share Plunge
items.summary: Nakamoto, led by Donald Trump's former crypto adviser David Bailey, sold 284 BTC at a 40% loss, with its shares down 99% over the past year.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: KPMG: AI Investment Gap Widening Between Spend and Business Value
items.summary: KPMG data reveals that while global AI investment is accelerating, the gap between enterprise AI spending and measurable business value is widening rapidly, highlighting challenges in translating AI investments into tangible returns.

items.title: Glia Wins Excellence Award for No-Hallucination Guarantee in Banking AI
items.summary: Glia has achieved a significant milestone by offering a no-hallucination guarantee for its banking AI platform, enabling fintechs to operate more safely with reduced AI-generated errors.

items.title: SAP and ANYbotics Drive Industrial Adoption of Physical AI
items.summary: ANYbotics' four-legged autonomous robots are being integrated directly into SAP's enterprise resource planning software, advancing physical AI adoption in industrial settings.

items.title: Google Announces Major AI Updates for March 2026
items.summary: Google expanded Gemini capabilities with improved context understanding, enhanced AI tools across Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Drive, upgraded Google Maps with conversational AI, and introduced tools to switch from competing AI platforms.

items.title: IBM and ETH Zurich Launch 10-Year AI-Quantum Computing Collaboration
items.summary: IBM and ETH Zurich announced a decade-long partnership to advance algorithms at the intersection of artificial intelligence and quantum computing, strengthening scientific collaboration in next-generation computing.

items.title: ElevenLabs and IBM Integrate Premium Voice Capabilities into Agentic AI
items.summary: ElevenLabs and IBM are collaborating to bring advanced Text-to-Speech and Speech-to-Text capabilities to IBM watsonx Orchestrate, enhancing natural voice interactions for enterprise agentic AI applications.

items.title: IBM Expands NVIDIA Collaboration to Operationalize Enterprise AI at Scale
items.summary: IBM announced expanded partnership with NVIDIA at GTC 2026 to help enterprises move AI from pilot to production through GPU-native analytics, intelligent document processing, and enterprise infrastructure solutions.

items.title: Latent Labs Announces Latent-Y: AI Agent for Therapeutic Antibody Design
items.summary: Latent-Y, an AI agent developed by Latent Labs, designs therapeutic antibodies from text prompts with a 67% target-level success rate, achieving single-digit nanomolar binding affinities without human intervention.

items.title: Amazon Reportedly Developing AI Smartphone Codenamed 'Transformer'
items.summary: Amazon is developing an AI-powered smartphone with Alexa integration, marking the company's return to the smartphone market over a decade after the failed Fire Phone launch.

items.title: Zanskar AI Models Accelerate Geothermal Discoveries
items.summary: Zanskar reports its AI models have made more geothermal discoveries in three years than the industry found in 30 years, targeting untapped U.S. energy sources.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Baldur's Gate 3 Developer Larian Studios Calls Out Crimson Desert
items.summary: Larian Studios, creators of the acclaimed Baldur's Gate 3, publicly criticized Pearl Abyss's Crimson Desert in a recent update, sparking discussion about the validity of their concerns.

items.title: Major Halo News: Sgt. Johnson Prequel Campaign and Campaign Evolved Details Revealed
items.summary: New official Halo announcements include details about a Sgt. Johnson prequel campaign and Campaign Evolved updates, featuring a never-before-seen Covenant faction with custom-built models.

items.title: Kena: Bridge of Spirits Launches on Nintendo Switch 2
items.summary: Kena: Bridge of Spirits is now available on Nintendo Switch 2, though early testing indicates it targets 30 FPS rather than 60 FPS, with mixed reception on the port quality.

items.title: RAID: Shadow Legends Brings Back Champion Skins and April Fools Content
items.summary: Plarium is reintroducing champion skins to RAID: Shadow Legends after an extended absence, alongside new April Fools cosmetics including a crowned chicken avatar.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Iran's IRGC Targets Major US Tech Companies in West Asia
items.summary: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps named 18 US tech firms including Microsoft, Apple, Google, Intel, IBM, Tesla, and Boeing as potential targets for their West Asia facilities, escalating tensions in modern warfare involving data centers and infrastructure.

items.title: SpaceX Files for Public Stock Offering
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX has filed papers with US regulators, paving the way for potentially the largest-ever public stock offering.

items.title: Thales Launches cortAIx AI Accelerator in Germany
items.summary: Thales has launched cortAIx, its AI accelerator, in Germany to drive AI applications for critical systems.

items.title: Apple Advances Global eSIM Connectivity
items.summary: Apple is making a significant leap in global eSIM technology, marking a turning point for mobile connectivity worldwide.

items.title: New Memory Chip Withstands Extreme High Temperatures
items.summary: Researchers developed a memory chip that survives temperatures hotter than lava, advancing hardware resilience.

items.title: Chip-Scale Light Tech for Faster Data Center Communications
items.summary: A new chip-scale light technology promises to power faster communications in AI and data centers.
